spec_match.rationale: The page now exercises native commandfor/command="request-close" buttons plus a spec-surface lab for reflected button.command, button.commandForElement, CommandEvent, and dialog.requestClose() with both normal and preventDefault() cancel paths.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the info dialog request-close path, form dialog input and Save path, guarded delete dialog and guard discard path, command reflection probe, requestClose() probe, prevented-cancel branch, and log clear control.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Page surfaces multiple failure / unsupported / edge-case branches in the UI, not just the happy path.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5592399713402880.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, button overrides keep background + color paired, and the edited form field no longer uses inline styles.

summary: Re-reviewed in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp. The page now links the ChromeStatus entry, demonstrates request-close through native command buttons, exposes the reflected command/commandForElement/CommandEvent/requestClose surfaces, and shows that requestClose() fires a cancellable cancel event. DevTools MCP verified info, form, guarded delete, command reflection, requestClose normal close, requestClose preventDefault, and conformance route; console was clean and network requests succeeded.
